---
title: "CGIAR Climate Data Hub - System-Wide Climate Data Asset Mapping"
subtitle: "Internal review draft"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: false
    embed-resources: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        #TOC {
          position: sticky;
          top: 1rem;
          max-height: calc(100vh - 2rem);
          overflow-y: auto;
        }
        @media print {
          details.code-fold summary,
          details.code-fold .sourceCode,
          details.code-fold .cell-output {
            display: none !important;
          }
        }
        /* Tables: smaller type, tight padding, columns sized to content so the
           last (definition / rationale) column gets the remaining width. */
        main table, .cell table, table {
          font-size: 0.80rem;
          width: 100%;
          table-layout: auto;
          border-collapse: collapse;
        }
        main table th, main table td,
        table th, table td {
          padding: 0.28rem 0.5rem;
          vertical-align: top;
          line-height: 1.3;
        }
        /* Collapse the leading label/numeric column (Domain, Centre, #, Rank)
           to its content width, leaving the rest for the wide text column. */
        table td:first-child, table th:first-child {
          white-space: nowrap;
          width: 1%;
        }
        /* The wide text column should wrap and absorb remaining width. */
        table td:last-child, table th:last-child {
          white-space: normal;
          width: auto;
        }
        /* Figure captions: tighter, smaller, secondary to the plot. */
        figure figcaption, .figure-caption, p > img + em {
          font-size: 0.82rem;
          color: #444;
          line-height: 1.35;
        }
        </style>
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
  docx:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
# This document is a reproducible notebook: load the normalised data and compute
# all statistics once, then generate every section, figure, and table from them.
import pathlib
import sys
import re
import random
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from IPython.display import Markdown, display
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from itables import init_notebook_mode, show

sys.path.insert(0, str(pathlib.Path.cwd() / "src"))
from report_common import build_report_body, compute_stats, load

assets, merge_log = load()
stats = compute_stats(assets, merge_log)
init_notebook_mode(all_interactive=True)

# ---- Shared report styling -------------------------------------------------
INK, GREEN, BLUE, BLUE_LT = "#0F1F2B", "#033529", "#1955A6", "#63A9FE"
ACCESS_COLOUR = {"Open": "#1F8A70", "Restricted": "#C2691C", "Unknown": "#7C8A99"}
SEG_PALETTE = ["#1955A6", "#1F8A70", "#E0A11B", "#63A9FE", "#C2691C", "#17B89B",
               "#7D8CC4", "#8B5CF6", "#2E7D52", "#EC4899", "#5C6B73", "#AE6C7A",
               "#4A90D9", "#F4B942"]
FONT = "IBM Plex Sans, Source Sans 3, system-ui, sans-serif"

def style(fig, height=460, **kw):
    fig.update_layout(template="simple_white", height=height,
                      font=dict(family=FONT, size=12, color=INK),
                      title_font=dict(family=FONT, color=GREEN),
                      paper_bgcolor="white", plot_bgcolor="white",
                      margin=dict(l=70, r=24, t=34, b=52), **kw)
    return fig

def _link(a):
    nm = a.get("name", "")
    return f'<a href="{a["url"]}" target="_blank">{nm}</a>' if a.get("url") else nm

def _why(a, n=300):
    return (a.get("justification") or a.get("primary_use_case") or a.get("short_description") or "")[:n]

def itab(df):
    show(df, classes="display compact", column_filters="header", paging=True,
         lengthMenu=[10, 25, 100], scrollX=True, allow_html=True, maxBytes=0,
         showIndex=False)

# Generate the narrative once, then split at the figure/table markers so the
# interactive figures and tables below are injected at the right positions.
body = build_report_body(assets, stats, figures_prefix="outputs/figures")
seg = re.split(r"<!--(?:FIG1|FIG6|TABLE1|QUADRANT|TABLE2|ANNEXA)-->", body)
seg += [""] * (7 - len(seg))   # guard if a marker is ever missing
```

**Version 1.0 (draft)** — `{python} stats["total"]` assets in this build. **Interactive data access:** [CDH interactive dashboard](docs/index.html).

> Generated programmatically from the data — no numbers are hand-typed. The figures and tables
> below are interactive (hover, sort, filter) in this HTML edition. See **Data Access, Feedback
> and Reproducibility** near the end for the source code, steps to reproduce, and how to suggest
> corrections or additions.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[0]))
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-centres
#| echo: false
# Figure 1 — assets per centre, each bar split into one segment per nominator.
by_centre = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(int))
totals = Counter()
for a in assets:
    nm = (a.get("nominator") or "Unattributed").split("\n")[0].strip() or "Unattributed"
    by_centre[a["centre"]][nm] += 1
    totals[a["centre"]] += 1
centres = sorted(totals, key=totals.get)        # ascending → largest on top
f1 = go.Figure()
for c in centres:
    base = 0
    for i, (nm, cnt) in enumerate(sorted(by_centre[c].items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])):
        f1.add_bar(y=[c.replace("_", "-")], x=[cnt], base=base, orientation="h",
                   width=0.7, marker_color=SEG_PALETTE[i % len(SEG_PALETTE)],
                   showlegend=False,
                   hovertemplate=f"<b>{nm}</b>: {cnt} asset(s)<extra></extra>")
        base += cnt
    f1.add_annotation(x=base, y=c.replace("_", "-"), text=str(base), showarrow=False,
                      xshift=11, font=dict(size=11, color=INK))
style(f1, barmode="overlay",
      xaxis=dict(title="Number of assets (bar split by nominator)"),
      yaxis=dict(categoryorder="array",
                 categoryarray=[c.replace("_", "-") for c in centres]))
f1
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[1]))
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-gap
#| echo: false
# Figure 6 — domain × geography coverage; hover for counts, red dot = gap.
DOMAINS = ["Hazard", "Hazard / Climate Services", "Exposure", "Sensitivity",
           "Adaptive Capacity", "Adaptation Analytics", "Mitigation",
           "Climate Policy / Finance", "Multi-domain"]
GEOS = ["Africa", "Asia / South & SE Asia", "Latin America & Caribbean",
        "Global", "Multi-regional"]
GEO_SHORT = ["Africa", "Asia /<br>SE Asia", "Lat. Am. /<br>Caribbean",
             "Global", "Multi-<br>regional"]
M = [[0] * len(GEOS) for _ in DOMAINS]
for a in assets:
    if a["domain_norm"] in DOMAINS and a["geo_norm"] in GEOS:
        M[DOMAINS.index(a["domain_norm"])][GEOS.index(a["geo_norm"])] += 1
f6 = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
    z=M, x=GEO_SHORT, y=DOMAINS, xgap=3, ygap=3, showscale=False,
    colorscale=[[0, "#FFFFFF"], [0.18, "#DCEBFB"], [0.55, BLUE_LT], [1, BLUE]],
    text=[[str(v) if v else "" for v in row] for row in M],
    texttemplate="%{text}", textfont=dict(size=11),
    hovertemplate="%{y} × %{x}: %{z} asset(s)<extra></extra>"))
for ri, row in enumerate(M):                     # red dot on true gaps
    for ci, v in enumerate(row):
        if v == 0:
            f6.add_annotation(x=GEO_SHORT[ci], y=DOMAINS[ri], text="•",
                              showarrow=False, font=dict(color="#D98273", size=13))
style(f6, height=430, xaxis=dict(side="top", tickfont=dict(size=10)),
      yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"))
f6
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[2]))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-strategic
#| echo: false
itab(pd.DataFrame([{
    "Centre": a["centre"].replace("_", "-"),
    "Rank": a.get("asset_rank") or "—",
    "Asset": _link(a),
    "Domain": a["domain_norm"],
    "Access": a["access_norm"],
    "Justification": _why(a),
} for a in stats["strategic"]]))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[3]))
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-quadrant
#| echo: false
# Figure 7 — readiness × reuse; colour = access, hover for the asset.
_jit = random.Random(42)
qfig = go.Figure()
for _acc, _col in ACCESS_COLOUR.items():
    _pts = [a for a in assets if a.get("access_norm") == _acc
            and a.get("score_components", {}).get("technical_readiness") is not None
            and a.get("score_components", {}).get("reuse_potential") is not None]
    qfig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=[a["score_components"]["technical_readiness"] + _jit.gauss(0, 0.018) for a in _pts],
        y=[a["score_components"]["reuse_potential"] + _jit.gauss(0, 0.018) for a in _pts],
        mode="markers", name=_acc,
        marker=dict(size=11, color=_col, opacity=0.72, line=dict(width=1, color="white")),
        customdata=[[a["name"], a["centre"], a["domain_norm"], _acc,
                     a.get("priority_score", "—")] for a in _pts],
        hovertemplate=("<b>%{customdata[0]}</b><br>%{customdata[1]} · %{customdata[2]}"
                       "<br>Access: %{customdata[3]} · Priority: %{customdata[4]}<extra></extra>"),
    ))
qfig.add_shape(type="rect", x0=0.7, y0=0.7, x1=1.06, y1=1.06,
               fillcolor="rgba(31,138,112,0.20)", line=dict(color="#1F8A70", width=1, dash="dot"),
               layer="below")
qfig.add_annotation(x=1.04, y=1.04, text="<b>Quick wins</b>", showarrow=False,
                    xanchor="right", font=dict(color="#1F8A70", size=12))
style(qfig, height=480, legend_title_text="Access",
      xaxis=dict(title="Technical readiness →", range=[0.15, 1.08]),
      yaxis=dict(title="Reuse potential →", range=[0.15, 1.08]))
qfig
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[4]))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-actnow
#| echo: false
itab(pd.DataFrame([{
    "Centre": a["centre"].replace("_", "-"),
    "Asset": _link(a),
    "Domain": a["domain_norm"],
    "Score": a.get("priority_score"),
    "Pathway": a.get("integration_hint", ""),
    "Rationale": _why(a),
} for a in stats["ingest_now"]]))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[5]))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-annexa
#| echo: false
itab(pd.DataFrame([{
    "Centre": a["centre"].replace("_", "-"),
    "Asset": _link(a),
    "Domain": a["domain_norm"],
    "Geography": a["geo_norm"],
    "Type": a.get("type_norm", ""),
    "Score": a.get("priority_score"),
    "Access": a.get("access_norm", ""),
    "Pathway": a.get("integration_hint", ""),
} for a in sorted(assets, key=lambda x: -(x.get("priority_score") or 0))]))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
display(Markdown(seg[6]))
```
